$description: Design guidelines extracted from Figma variable descriptions. Only tokens with non-empty descriptions are included.

25.purpose: The lightest step in the teal primitive scale; a near-white tinted surface value.
25.usage: Referenced by semantic tokens requiring a very subtle teal-tinted wash, such as colour/state/selected in light mode.
25.guidelines: Do not apply directly to component design. All usage must flow through a semantic alias. Reserve for contexts where the tint is barely perceptible against a white background.

50.purpose: A soft, low-saturation teal primitive establishing the second lightest step in the scale.
50.usage: Consumed by semantic tokens that require a light teal background, such as state and hover overlays.
50.guidelines: Not for direct use in layouts or components. Should always be aliased through a semantic token.

100.purpose: A light teal primitive representing the third step of the scale.
100.usage: Available for aliasing into semantic tokens where a light but visible teal fill is required.
100.guidelines: Use only as a primitive source value. Do not hard-code into component styles.

200.purpose: A medium-light teal primitive at the fourth step of the scale.
200.usage: Provides a mid-range light value for teal; usable as a source for subtle border or tinted fill tokens.
200.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Must be consumed via a semantic token alias.

300.purpose: A mid-range teal primitive establishing a clearly visible, unsaturated teal.
300.usage: Aliased into semantic tokens for interactive borders, icon fills, and link text in dark mode contexts.
300.guidelines: Not for direct application. Check contrast ratio when aliasing to text or icon tokens — minimum 4.5:1 required for WCAG AA compliance.

400.purpose: A medium teal primitive at the fifth-brightest step of the scale.
400.usage: Source value for semantic action, icon, and interactive element tokens in dark mode.
400.guidelines: Validate contrast when used as foreground on dark surfaces. Not for direct use in component design.

500.purpose: The core brand teal; the primary mid-point of the scale and the anchor for the brand identity.
500.usage: Aliased into colour/action/primary (light mode) and colour/background/brand. The single brand-colour source value provided by the brand designer.
500.guidelines: Treat as the canonical brand colour. When aliasing to text or icon contexts, verify WCAG AA compliance. Do not substitute other primitives where teal/500 is expected.

600.purpose: A darkened brand teal primitive one step below the mid-point.
600.usage: Aliased into hover state tokens for primary actions in light mode (colour/action/primary/hover).
600.guidelines: Use to communicate interaction depth on primary actions. Maintain at least one step of contrast against the resting-state primitive.

700.purpose: A dark teal primitive providing strong contrast against light surfaces.
700.usage: Aliased into pressed/active state tokens for primary actions in light mode, and into colour/background/promo.
700.guidelines: Reserved for pressed states and promotional backgrounds. Do not use where a softer brand presence is required.

800.purpose: A very dark teal primitive approaching near-black saturation.
800.usage: Available for aliasing into dark, high-contrast teal surfaces such as deep brand backgrounds.
800.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Ensure any foreground aliased against this value meets 4.5:1 contrast for text.

900.purpose: The darkest teal near the bottom of the scale, suitable for deep inverse backgrounds.
900.usage: Aliased into colour/background/inverse to provide the dark branded surface in light mode.
900.guidelines: Only use in contexts where maximum teal depth is required. Foreground on this surface must be a near-white neutral.

950.purpose: The absolute darkest step of the teal scale; near-black with a teal undertone.
950.usage: Available as a source value for the very deepest teal surfaces if required by future component needs.
950.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Use sparingly — prefer teal/900 unless maximum depth is explicitly required.

0.purpose: The zero-point anchor of the neutral scale; pure white in light mode and the deepest dark-mode base in dark mode.
0.usage: Aliased into colour/background/default and colour/background/raised in light mode; inverted to the darkest surface in dark mode.
0.guidelines: A special-case primitive. Never substitute another step where neutral/0 is expected, as it carries structural meaning as both the lightest and darkest extremes depending on mode.

50.purpose: The lightest non-white step of the neutral scale; a very subtle off-white.
50.usage: Aliased into colour/background/subtle and colour/background/surface to distinguish page-level tinted regions and component backgrounds from the default canvas.
50.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Note that subtle and surface share this primitive intentionally — semantic differentiation is achieved through borders and elevation, not colour alone.

100.purpose: A light neutral primitive providing a soft, visible background distinction.
100.usage: Aliased into colour/background/muted, colour/border/subtle, and colour/state/disabled/bg.
100.guidelines: Not for direct use. When aliased to border tokens, ensure visual separation from adjacent surfaces is sufficient without relying on this colour alone.

200.purpose: A slightly darker light neutral, providing a clearly visible but low-contrast value.
200.usage: Aliased into colour/border/default and colour/action/secondary for default borders and secondary action fills.
200.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Border tokens aliasing this value must maintain a minimum 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent surfaces per WCAG AA.

300.purpose: A mid-light neutral sitting at a clearly visible step between light and mid tones.
300.guidelines: Not for direct use. When aliased to interactive controls, verify that hover states provide sufficient visual change from the resting state.

400.purpose: A mid neutral at the boundary between light and medium tones.
400.usage: Aliased into colour/text/tertiary (light mode), colour/state/disabled/text, and colour/action/secondary/pressed.
400.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. When aliased to text tokens, ensure the resulting combination meets WCAG AA contrast against its background context.

500.purpose: The true mid-point of the neutral scale; a balanced grey with equal contrast in both directions.
500.usage: Aliased into colour/border/subtle and colour/onBackground/muted in light mode.
500.guidelines: Not for direct use. The mirror relationship between neutral/500 and neutral/400 across modes is intentional — validate both mode outputs when modifying this token.

600.purpose: A mid-dark neutral providing stronger contrast than the mid-point.
600.usage: Aliased into colour/text/tertiary and colour/onBackground/subtle in light mode.
600.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm WCAG AA compliance (4.5:1 for body text) against all background contexts where this value is applied via semantic aliases.

700.purpose: A dark neutral close to the deep anchor, suitable for icon and secondary heading values.
700.usage: Aliased into colour/text/secondary and colour/icon/default to ensure icons read clearly against light surfaces.
700.guidelines: Not for direct use. As an icon colour source, the semantic token should maintain 3:1 contrast against the surface it appears on.

800.purpose: A very dark neutral near the bottom of the light-mode scale.
800.usage: Aliased into component-layer tokens and dark-mode background tokens requiring high-contrast dark fills.
800.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Use to support elevation distinctions in dark mode through subtle tonal differences.

900.purpose: The near-darkest neutral in the scale; a deep, ink-like tone for primary text and strong foreground elements.
900.usage: Aliased into colour/text/primary and colour/onBackground/default in light mode.
900.guidelines: Not for direct use. Always verify 7:1 contrast against the default background for AAA compliance, and minimum 4.5:1 for AA.

950.purpose: The darkest step in the neutral scale; the primary dark-mode base surface colour.
950.usage: Aliased into colour/background/default in dark mode and colour/text/inverse in light mode.
950.guidelines: A structural anchor primitive. Changes to this value propagate broadly — always audit all downstream semantic aliases before modifying.

50.purpose: The lightest step of the green (success) primitive scale.
50.usage: Aliased into colour/background/success in light mode.
50.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Preserve the structural pairing with green/900 for dark-mode aliasing.

100.purpose: Second-lightest green primitive; a light tinted green surface value.
100.usage: Available for aliasing into additional success-status surface tokens if required.
100.guidelines: Not currently mapped to an active semantic token. Primitive-layer only.

200.purpose: Third-step green primitive at a clearly visible light tint.
200.usage: Reserved for future aliasing or extended success-status UI patterns.
200.guidelines: Not for direct use. Only alias if extending the success palette beyond current semantic coverage.

300.purpose: Mid-light green primitive providing a visible, saturated border value.
300.usage: Aliased into colour/border/success in dark mode and colour/onBackground/success in dark mode.
300.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm 3:1 contrast against success background when applied via the border semantic token.

400.purpose: Medium green primitive above the mid-point of the success scale.
400.usage: Aliased into colour/icon/success and colour/border/success in dark mode.
400.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify icon contrast (3:1 minimum) against the dark-mode success background.

500.purpose: Core mid-point of the green scale; the primary success green.
500.usage: Available for aliasing into interactive or prominent success-status UI elements.
500.guidelines: Not currently mapped to an active semantic token. If aliased to text, verify WCAG AA compliance.

600.purpose: Dark-leaning green providing strong contrast on light success surfaces.
600.usage: Aliased into colour/icon/success and colour/border/success in light mode.
600.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm 3:1 icon contrast against colour/background/success when applied.

700.purpose: Dark green with high legibility on light success backgrounds.
700.usage: Aliased into colour/onBackground/success in light mode for text and foreground content on success surfaces.
700.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify 4.5:1 contrast against the light-mode success background.

800.purpose: Very dark green near the bottom of the scale.
800.usage: Available for deeper dark-mode or high-contrast success surfaces if required.
800.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Not currently mapped. Only alias if extending the success palette.

900.purpose: Darkest step of the green scale; near-black with a green undertone.
900.usage: Aliased into colour/background/success in dark mode.
900.guidelines: Not for direct use. Maintains structural pairing with green/50 for cross-mode symmetry.

50.purpose: The lightest step of the red (error) primitive scale.
50.usage: Aliased into colour/background/error in light mode.
50.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Preserve the structural pairing with red/900 for dark-mode aliasing consistency.

100.purpose: Second-lightest red; a soft, low-saturation error-tinted surface.
100.usage: Available for aliasing into additional error-status surface tokens if required.

200.purpose: Third-step red primitive at a clearly visible light tint.
200.usage: Reserved for future aliasing or extended error-status UI patterns.

300.purpose: Mid-light red primitive for visible error-status borders.
300.usage: Aliased into colour/onBackground/error in dark mode.
300.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Ensure 3:1 contrast against the error background when applied via the semantic border token.

400.purpose: Medium red above the scale mid-point.
400.usage: Aliased into colour/border/error and colour/icon/error in dark mode.
400.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify 3:1 icon contrast against the dark-mode error background.

500.purpose: Core mid-point of the red scale; the primary error red.
500.usage: Available for aliasing into prominent or interactive error-status UI elements if required.
500.guidelines: Not currently mapped. If aliased to text, verify WCAG AA compliance.

600.purpose: Dark-leaning red with high contrast on light error surfaces.
600.usage: Aliased into colour/icon/error and colour/border/error in light mode.
600.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm 3:1 icon contrast against colour/background/error.

700.purpose: Dark red with strong contrast on light error backgrounds.
700.usage: Aliased into colour/onBackground/error in light mode.
700.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify 4.5:1 contrast against the light-mode error background.

800.purpose: Very dark red near the bottom of the scale.
800.usage: Available for additional dark error surfaces if required.

900.purpose: Darkest step of the red scale; near-black with a red undertone.
900.usage: Aliased into colour/background/error in dark mode.
900.guidelines: Not for direct use. Maintains structural pairing with red/50 for cross-mode symmetry.

50.purpose: The lightest step of the amber (warning) primitive scale.
50.usage: Aliased into colour/background/warning in light mode.
50.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Preserve pairing with amber/900 for dark-mode structural consistency.

100.purpose: Second-lightest amber; a soft, warm-tinted surface value.
100.usage: Available for aliasing into additional warning-status surface tokens if required.

200.purpose: Third-step amber at a clearly visible warm tint.
200.usage: Aliased into colour/onBackground/warning in dark mode.

300.purpose: Mid-light amber primitive for visible warning borders.
300.usage: Aliased into colour/border/warning and colour/icon/warning in dark mode.
300.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm 3:1 contrast against the warning background when applied via the semantic border token.

400.purpose: Medium amber above the scale mid-point.
400.usage: Aliased into colour/border/warning in dark mode.
400.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify 3:1 icon contrast against the dark-mode warning background.

500.purpose: Core mid-point of the amber scale; the primary warning amber.
500.usage: Available for aliasing into prominent or interactive warning-status elements if required.
500.guidelines: Not currently mapped. If aliased to text, verify WCAG AA compliance — amber hues are notoriously low-contrast on white.

600.purpose: Dark-leaning amber with improved contrast on light warning surfaces.
600.usage: Aliased into colour/icon/warning in light mode.
600.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm 3:1 icon contrast against colour/background/warning.

700.purpose: Dark amber with strong contrast on light warning backgrounds.
700.usage: Aliased into colour/border/warning and colour/icon/warning in light mode.
700.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify 4.5:1 contrast against the light-mode warning background. Amber can fail AA at lighter values — confirm explicitly.

800.purpose: Very dark amber near the bottom of the scale.
800.usage: Aliased into colour/onBackground/warning in light mode.
800.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Not currently mapped beyond onBackground/warning.

900.purpose: Darkest step of the amber scale.
900.usage: Aliased into colour/background/warning in dark mode.
900.guidelines: Not for direct use. Maintains structural pairing with amber/50 for cross-mode symmetry.

50.purpose: The lightest step of the blue (info) primitive scale.
50.usage: Aliased into colour/background/info in light mode.
50.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Paired with blue/900 as its dark-mode counterpart.

100.purpose: A light blue primitive at the second step of the info scale.
100.usage: Available for aliasing into light-tinted info UI surfaces if additional steps are required.
100.guidelines: Not currently mapped to an active semantic token. Primitive-layer only.

200.purpose: Third-step blue primitive at a clearly visible light tint.
200.usage: Reserved for future aliasing into extended info-status UI patterns.

300.purpose: Mid-light blue providing a visible info-status border value.
300.usage: Available for aliasing into info border or icon tokens in dark mode if required.

400.purpose: Medium blue above the mid-point of the info scale.
400.usage: Aliased into colour/onBackground/info and colour/border/info in dark mode.
400.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify 3:1 contrast against the dark-mode info background.

500.purpose: Core mid-point of the blue scale; the primary info blue.
500.usage: Available for aliasing into prominent or interactive info-status UI elements.
500.guidelines: Not currently mapped. If aliased to text, verify WCAG AA compliance.

600.purpose: Dark-leaning blue with strong contrast on light info surfaces.
600.usage: Aliased into colour/border/info and colour/icon/info in light mode.
600.guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm 3:1 contrast against colour/background/info.

700.purpose: Dark blue with high legibility on light info backgrounds.
700.usage: Aliased into colour/onBackground/info in light mode.
700.guidelines: Not for direct use. Verify 4.5:1 contrast against the light-mode info background.

800.purpose: Very dark blue near the bottom of the scale.
800.usage: Available for additional dark info surfaces if required.

900.purpose: Darkest step of the blue scale; deep navy with a blue undertone.
900.usage: Aliased into colour/background/info in dark mode.
900.guidelines: Not for direct use. Maintains structural pairing with blue/50 for cross-mode symmetry.
